jobs.prompt: 16:9 educational editorial illustration for a university probability theory lecture on conditional probability, Bayes' theorem, and independence. Show a large warm sample space region with many soft scattered points, and one highlighted sub-region B (dusty blue), inside which a smaller overlap area is marked in terracotta. The focal idea is: restricting attention to B and measuring the fraction that falls in A. One main idea only. Warm paper background, deep ink contours, premium academic textbook style, generous whitespace. No readable text, no formulas, no digits.
